contentBlocks.original:
श्रियः कांताय कल्याण निधये निधयेर्थिनाम् ।
श्रीवेंकटनिवासाय श्रीनिवासाय मंगलम् ।।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śriyaḥ kāṃtāya kalyāṇa nidhaye nidhayerthinām .
śrīveṃkaṭanivāsāya śrīnivāsāya maṃgalam ..
contentBlocks.translation: O auspicious Lord Srinivasa, the auspiciously-wonderful-bodied god, the bestower of all desired wishes, the consort of Sri (Lakshmi), the depository of the treasure of auspiciousness, the wealthy treasure of beseechers, resident of Venkatagiri, salutations to that Lord Srinivasa.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रियः (śriyaḥ) – of Sri (Lakshmi); कांताय (kāntāya) – to the consort; कल्याण (kalyāṇa) – auspicious; निधये (nidhaye) – to the treasure; निधयेर्थिनाम् (nidhayerthinām) – treasure of beseechers (nidhaye:to the treasure | arthinām:of beseechers); श्रीवेंकटनिवासाय (śrīveṃkaṭanivāsāya) – to the resident of Venkatagiri (śrī:auspicious | veṃkaṭa:Venkatagiri | nivāsāya:to the resident); श्रीनिवासाय (śrīnivāsāya) – to Lord Srinivasa (śrī:auspicious | nivāsāya:to the resident); मंगलम् (maṃgalam) – salutations;
